forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: c680
+title: 'Bug: Cannot launch command palette — not in menu, not by hotkey'
+---
+## What
+Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened — neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut.
+
+The palette opener command is `ui.palette.open` (cua `Mod+K`, vim `:`) — see `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts:864,1062`. The palette overlay itself is `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`, rendered in `app-shell.tsx` gated on `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671,818). Backend state is `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` (`apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1831`).
+
+Two distinct failure surfaces to investigate:
+1. **Hotkey path**: the keybindings tests note `Mod+K`/`:` only fire "when a scope claims it" (keybindings.test.ts:1062–1097). If no focused scope surfaces `ui.palette.open`, the binding never resolves. Determine which scope is expected to claim `ui.palette.open` and why it isn't at runtime.
+2. **Menu path**: `ui.palette.open` appears to have no `menu` placement, so `collect_menu_entries` (`menu.rs:88`) never adds it to any native submenu — there is no menu affordance at all. Decide the correct menu home (likely View or App) and add the placement, OR confirm the intended affordance.
+
+NOTE: likely shares a root cause with the blank-Navigation-menu bug and the jump-to-inspect bug — focus/command surfacing at runtime. Cross-check before fixing in isolation.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `Mod+K` (cua) / `:` (vim) opens the command palette from a normal board focus.
+- [ ] There is a discoverable native-menu affordance that opens the palette.
+- [ ] Root cause documented (scope not claiming the command vs. binding resolution vs. missing menu placement).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` to assert `Mod+K`/`:` dispatches `ui.palette.open` under the real board scope chain (not a synthetic single-scope fixture).
+- [ ] If a menu placement is added: a `menu.rs` test asserting `ui.palette.open` collects into its intended submenu key.
+- [ ] Regression test failing before, passing after.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — failing test first, then fix.
\ No newline at end of file

-title: 'Bug: Cannot launch command palette — not in menu, not by hotkey'
----
-## What
-Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened — neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut.
-
-The palette opener command is `ui.palette.open` (cua `Mod+K`, vim `:`) — see `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts:864,1062`. The palette overlay itself is `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`, rendered in `app-shell.tsx` gated on `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671,818). Backend state is `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` (`apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1831`).
-
-Two distinct failure surfaces to investigate:
-1. **Hotkey path**: the keybindings tests note `Mod+K`/`:` only fire "when a scope claims it" (keybindings.test.ts:1062–1097). If no focused scope surfaces `ui.palette.open`, the binding never resolves. Determine which scope is expected to claim `ui.palette.open` and why it isn't at runtime.
-2. **Menu path**: `ui.palette.open` appears to have no `menu` placement, so `collect_menu_entries` (`menu.rs:88`) never adds it to any native submenu — there is no menu affordance at all. Decide the correct menu home (likely View or App) and add the placement, OR confirm the intended affordance.
-
-NOTE: likely shares a root cause with the blank-Navigation-menu bug and the jump-to-inspect bug — focus/command surfacing at runtime. Cross-check before fixing in isolation.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `Mod+K` (cua) / `:` (vim) opens the command palette from a normal board focus.
-- [ ] There is a discoverable native-menu affordance that opens the palette.
-- [ ] Root cause documented (scope not claiming the command vs. binding resolution vs. missing menu placement).
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` to assert `Mod+K`/`:` dispatches `ui.palette.open` under the real board scope chain (not a synthetic single-scope fixture).
-- [ ] If a menu placement is added: a `menu.rs` test asserting `ui.palette.open` collects into its intended submenu key.
-- [ ] Regression test failing before, passing after.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — failing test first, then fix.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,25 +6,41 @@
 title: 'Bug: Cannot launch command palette — not in menu, not by hotkey'
 ---
 ## What
-Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened — neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut.
+Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened — neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut. (User pressed a hotkey and nothing happened.)
+
+## It is NOT a window — it's an overlay React component
+`<CommandPalette>` is rendered in `app-shell.tsx:818-826` as `<FocusLayer name="palette"><CommandPalette/></FocusLayer>`, **portaled to `document.body`** (`createPortal`, see comment app-shell.tsx:741-746). It is conditionally mounted only when `paletteOpen` is true. `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671) — the **per-window backend UIState flag**. So the open path is: command → backend sets `palette_open=true` → `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` → `ui-state-context` updates `windows[label].palette_open` → AppShell re-renders → overlay mounts. No OS window involved.
+
+## Root cause (investigated): three-way divergence in the open path + no menu entry
+There are **three** palette-open command IDs, all wired to backend handlers, but the frontend keybindings only listen for one of them:
+
+| Command ID | Backend handler (`commands/mod.rs`) | Declared keys | Frontend listens? |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `ui.palette.open` | `PaletteOpenCmd` (mod.rs:150) | `ui.yaml`: cua **Mod+K**, vim `:` | ❌ NOT in `BINDING_TABLES`, NOT in any frontend scope |
+| `app.command` | `CommandPaletteCmd` (mod.rs:310) | `app.yaml`: vim `:`, cua/emacs **Mod+Shift+P** | ✅ vim `:` → `app.command` |
+| `app.palette` | `CommandPaletteCmd` alias (mod.rs:315) | **Mod+Shift+P** all modes | ✅ `Mod+Shift+P` → `app.palette` |

-The palette opener command is `ui.palette.open` (cua `Mod+K`, vim `:`) — see `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts:864,1062`. The palette overlay itself is `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`, rendered in `app-shell.tsx` gated on `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671,818). Backend state is `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` (`apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1831`).
+- Frontend static `BINDING_TABLES` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts:27-140`) only bind `Mod+Shift+P → app.palette` (all modes) and vim `:` → `app.command`.
+- The frontend global command set (`STATIC_GLOBAL_COMMANDS`, app-shell.tsx:170-180) contains `app.command` and `app.palette` — but **NOT `ui.palette.open`**. So `extractScopeBindings` never surfaces `ui.palette.open`, and `Mod+K` is not in `BINDING_TABLES` → **`Mod+K` resolves to nothing in the live app.**
+- No palette command (`ui.palette.open` / `app.command` / `app.palette`) carries a `menu:` placement → **no native menu affordance at all** (matches "not on the menu").

-Two distinct failure surfaces to investigate:
-1. **Hotkey path**: the keybindings tests note `Mod+K`/`:` only fire "when a scope claims it" (keybindings.test.ts:1062–1097). If no focused scope surfaces `ui.palette.open`, the binding never resolves. Determine which scope is expected to claim `ui.palette.open` and why it isn't at runtime.
-2. **Menu path**: `ui.palette.open` appears to have no `menu` placement, so `collect_menu_entries` (`menu.rs:88`) never adds it to any native submenu — there is no menu affordance at all. Decide the correct menu home (likely View or App) and add the placement, OR confirm the intended affordance.
+**Conclusion:** the registry/menu world advertises `ui.palette.open` / `Mod+K`, but the running frontend only opens the palette via **`Mod+Shift+P`** (or vim `:`). The user almost certainly pressed the advertised `Mod+K`, which is dead on the frontend.

-NOTE: likely shares a root cause with the blank-Navigation-menu bug and the jump-to-inspect bug — focus/command surfacing at runtime. Cross-check before fixing in isolation.
+## First implementer step — bisect A vs B
+1. **A (most likely):** `Mod+Shift+P` DOES open the palette → the bug is the keybinding/menu divergence. Fix by consolidating to ONE palette command (prefer `ui.palette.open`), wiring its key into `BINDING_TABLES` / the frontend global scope, deleting the `app.command`/`app.palette` duplication, and adding a `menu:` placement (View or App) so there's a clickable affordance.
+2. **B (deeper):** if `Mod+Shift+P` ALSO does nothing, the failure is downstream — `palette_open` not propagating (`ui-state-context`), the `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` envelope not unwrapping (cf. the `{ok,change}` envelope issue in recent commit af9e6e965), or the `<FocusLayer parentLayerFq>` mount being rejected. Capture `log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"'` while pressing the key.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `Mod+K` (cua) / `:` (vim) opens the command palette from a normal board focus.
+- [ ] A single documented shortcut opens the palette in all keymap modes, and it matches what the registry/menu advertise (no Mod+K vs Mod+Shift+P divergence).
 - [ ] There is a discoverable native-menu affordance that opens the palette.
-- [ ] Root cause documented (scope not claiming the command vs. binding resolution vs. missing menu placement).
+- [ ] The three-way `ui.palette.open` / `app.command` / `app.palette` duplication is reconciled to a single source of truth (or intentional aliases that all share one binding wired into the frontend).
+- [ ] Root cause confirmed as A or B (see bisect step) and documented.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` to assert `Mod+K`/`:` dispatches `ui.palette.open` under the real board scope chain (not a synthetic single-scope fixture).
-- [ ] If a menu placement is added: a `menu.rs` test asserting `ui.palette.open` collects into its intended submenu key.
-- [ ] Regression test failing before, passing after.
+- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` so the canonical palette shortcut resolves to the canonical palette command under the real board scope chain.
+- [ ] A `menu.rs` test asserting the palette command collects into its intended submenu key (once a `menu:` placement is added).
+- [ ] If B: a `ui-state-context` test that a `PaletteOpen` change flips `windows[label].palette_open` and mounts the overlay.
+- [ ] Regression test failing before the fix, passing after.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — failing test first, then fix. #bug
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — failing test first, then fix.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,41 +6,45 @@
 title: 'Bug: Cannot launch command palette — not in menu, not by hotkey'
 ---
 ## What
-Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened — neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut. (User pressed a hotkey and nothing happened.)
+Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened — not from the menu, not by a hotkey (user pressed the advertised `Mod+K`, nothing happened).

-## It is NOT a window — it's an overlay React component
-`<CommandPalette>` is rendered in `app-shell.tsx:818-826` as `<FocusLayer name="palette"><CommandPalette/></FocusLayer>`, **portaled to `document.body`** (`createPortal`, see comment app-shell.tsx:741-746). It is conditionally mounted only when `paletteOpen` is true. `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671) — the **per-window backend UIState flag**. So the open path is: command → backend sets `palette_open=true` → `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` → `ui-state-context` updates `windows[label].palette_open` → AppShell re-renders → overlay mounts. No OS window involved.
+## The palette is an overlay, not a window
+`<CommandPalette>` mounts via `createPortal(document.body)` inside `<FocusLayer name="palette">`, gated on a per-window `palette_open` flag. No OS window is involved. The open path is: command → service sets `palette_open=true` → UI-state change event → React re-renders → overlay mounts.

-## Root cause (investigated): three-way divergence in the open path + no menu entry
-There are **three** palette-open command IDs, all wired to backend handlers, but the frontend keybindings only listen for one of them:
+## Current state (this repo — `swissarmyhammer-plugin`)
+The palette-open commands are ALREADY TS plugin commands (good — not Rust handlers), but there are **four** of them and the flag lives on the `ui_state` service, and the live frontend keymap binds a different key than the registry advertises:

-| Command ID | Backend handler (`commands/mod.rs`) | Declared keys | Frontend listens? |
+| Command (TS plugin) | File | Keys | Routes to |
 |---|---|---|---|
-| `ui.palette.open` | `PaletteOpenCmd` (mod.rs:150) | `ui.yaml`: cua **Mod+K**, vim `:` | ❌ NOT in `BINDING_TABLES`, NOT in any frontend scope |
-| `app.command` | `CommandPaletteCmd` (mod.rs:310) | `app.yaml`: vim `:`, cua/emacs **Mod+Shift+P** | ✅ vim `:` → `app.command` |
-| `app.palette` | `CommandPaletteCmd` alias (mod.rs:315) | **Mod+Shift+P** all modes | ✅ `Mod+Shift+P` → `app.palette` |
+| `app.command` | `builtin/plugins/app-shell-commands/commands/app.ts:65` | vim `:`, cua/emacs `Mod+Shift+P` | `ui_state…command.show` (command mode) |
+| `app.palette` (visible:false) | `app.ts:81` | `Mod+Shift+P` all modes | `ui_state…palette.show` |
+| `app.search` | `app.ts:98` | vim `/`, cua/emacs `Mod+F` | `ui_state…search.show` (search mode) |
+| `ui.palette.open` | `builtin/plugins/ui-commands/index.ts:221` | cua `Mod+K`, vim `:` | `ui_state…palette.open` |
+| `ui.palette.close` | `ui-commands/index.ts:235` | — | `ui_state…palette.close` |

-- Frontend static `BINDING_TABLES` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts:27-140`) only bind `Mod+Shift+P → app.palette` (all modes) and vim `:` → `app.command`.
-- The frontend global command set (`STATIC_GLOBAL_COMMANDS`, app-shell.tsx:170-180) contains `app.command` and `app.palette` — but **NOT `ui.palette.open`**. So `extractScopeBindings` never surfaces `ui.palette.open`, and `Mod+K` is not in `BINDING_TABLES` → **`Mod+K` resolves to nothing in the live app.**
-- No palette command (`ui.palette.open` / `app.command` / `app.palette`) carries a `menu:` placement → **no native menu affordance at all** (matches "not on the menu").
+The flag and its ops live on **`swissarmyhammer-ui-state`** (`crates/swissarmyhammer-ui-state/src/lib.rs`). `Mod+K` resolves to nothing in the running frontend because the live keymap only binds `Mod+Shift+P`/`:`/`/` — so the registry advertises a key the app never listens for, and no palette command carries a `menu:` placement (→ "not on the menu").

-**Conclusion:** the registry/menu world advertises `ui.palette.open` / `Mod+K`, but the running frontend only opens the palette via **`Mod+Shift+P`** (or vim `:`). The user almost certainly pressed the advertised `Mod+K`, which is dead on the frontend.
-
-## First implementer step — bisect A vs B
-1. **A (most likely):** `Mod+Shift+P` DOES open the palette → the bug is the keybinding/menu divergence. Fix by consolidating to ONE palette command (prefer `ui.palette.open`), wiring its key into `BINDING_TABLES` / the frontend global scope, deleting the `app.command`/`app.palette` duplication, and adding a `menu:` placement (View or App) so there's a clickable affordance.
-2. **B (deeper):** if `Mod+Shift+P` ALSO does nothing, the failure is downstream — `palette_open` not propagating (`ui-state-context`), the `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` envelope not unwrapping (cf. the `{ok,change}` envelope issue in recent commit af9e6e965), or the `<FocusLayer parentLayerFq>` mount being rejected. Capture `log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"'` while pressing the key.
+## DECIDED DESIGN (from user)
+1. **One and only one command** opens the command palette. Collapse `app.command`, `app.palette`, and `ui.palette.open` into a SINGLE command id (recommend keeping `ui.palette.open` as the survivor — it's the semantically correct, menu-world identity — and deleting `app.command` + `app.palette`). Open sub-decision: `app.search` opens the palette in *search* mode — keep it as a distinct command but it MUST route through the same single service flag (mode is a parameter, not a second flag). Flag this when implementing.
+2. **TS command registered as a plugin — never a Rust backend handler.** The survivor lives as a builtin TS plugin command (in `builtin/plugins/...`). Delete any remaining Rust `Command` impls for the palette (legacy `PaletteOpenCmd` / `CommandPaletteCmd` / `SearchPaletteCmd` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/{ui,app}_commands.rs`) so there is exactly one implementation path.
+3. **The `palette_open` flag lives on ONE plugin service — the `window` service** (`swissarmyhammer-window-service`), per user (open to alternatives; `ui_state` is the incumbent owner). Move per-window palette visibility (`palette_open` + `palette_mode`) out of `swissarmyhammer-ui-state` and onto the `window` service as a per-window field with `open palette` / `close palette` (+ mode) ops, so the single TS command and the React reader both read/write ONE service.
+   - Tradeoff to weigh during impl: `ui_state` already owns the other per-window UI-surface flags (inspector stack, keymap mode). Putting palette on `window` splits per-window UI state across two services. If that split is undesirable, the single-owner could instead be `ui_state` (which is already singular) — the hard requirement is ONE owning service, not which. Confirm window vs ui_state before moving state.
+4. **Wire the one command's key into the live keymap** so it actually fires (the root cause of "not by a hotkey"): the survivor's `keys` must drive the real keybinding handler — no separate static binding table that diverges. Pick ONE canonical shortcut and make registry + keymap + menu agree.
+5. **Add a `menu:` placement** to the survivor so there is a discoverable menu affordance (fixes "not on the menu").

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A single documented shortcut opens the palette in all keymap modes, and it matches what the registry/menu advertise (no Mod+K vs Mod+Shift+P divergence).
-- [ ] There is a discoverable native-menu affordance that opens the palette.
-- [ ] The three-way `ui.palette.open` / `app.command` / `app.palette` duplication is reconciled to a single source of truth (or intentional aliases that all share one binding wired into the frontend).
-- [ ] Root cause confirmed as A or B (see bisect step) and documented.
+- [ ] Exactly one command opens the command palette (the `app.command`/`app.palette`/`ui.palette.open` triplet is reduced to one; `app.search` only differs by mode and shares the single flag).
+- [ ] That command is a TS plugin command; no Rust `Command` impl for palette open/close remains.
+- [ ] `palette_open` (+ `palette_mode`) is owned by a single plugin service (window per user, or ui_state if the split is rejected) — not duplicated across services.
+- [ ] One canonical shortcut opens the palette in all keymap modes, and registry + live keymap + menu all agree on it.
+- [ ] A native-menu affordance opens the palette.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` so the canonical palette shortcut resolves to the canonical palette command under the real board scope chain.
-- [ ] A `menu.rs` test asserting the palette command collects into its intended submenu key (once a `menu:` placement is added).
-- [ ] If B: a `ui-state-context` test that a `PaletteOpen` change flips `windows[label].palette_open` and mounts the overlay.
+- [ ] Update `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_app_shell_commands_e2e.rs` and `builtin_ui_commands_e2e.rs` for the collapsed command set (the current test asserts the old triplet incl. `app.palette` — it must change to assert the single survivor).
+- [ ] Service test on the chosen owner (`swissarmyhammer-window-service` or `swissarmyhammer-ui-state`): `open palette` sets the per-window flag and emits the change; `close palette`/`dismiss` clears it.
+- [ ] Frontend keymap test: the canonical shortcut resolves to the single palette command under the real board scope chain (no Mod+K vs Mod+Shift+P divergence).
+- [ ] Menu test: the palette command collects into its intended submenu.
 - [ ] Regression test failing before the fix, passing after.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — failing test first, then fix.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — failing test first, then implement.
\ No newline at end of file
reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,45 +6,41 @@
 title: 'Bug: Cannot launch command palette — not in menu, not by hotkey'
 ---
 ## What
-Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened — not from the menu, not by a hotkey (user pressed the advertised `Mod+K`, nothing happened).
+Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened — neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut. (User pressed a hotkey and nothing happened.)

-## The palette is an overlay, not a window
-`<CommandPalette>` mounts via `createPortal(document.body)` inside `<FocusLayer name="palette">`, gated on a per-window `palette_open` flag. No OS window is involved. The open path is: command → service sets `palette_open=true` → UI-state change event → React re-renders → overlay mounts.
+## It is NOT a window — it's an overlay React component
+`<CommandPalette>` is rendered in `app-shell.tsx:818-826` as `<FocusLayer name="palette"><CommandPalette/></FocusLayer>`, **portaled to `document.body`** (`createPortal`, see comment app-shell.tsx:741-746). It is conditionally mounted only when `paletteOpen` is true. `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671) — the **per-window backend UIState flag**. So the open path is: command → backend sets `palette_open=true` → `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` → `ui-state-context` updates `windows[label].palette_open` → AppShell re-renders → overlay mounts. No OS window involved.

-## Current state (this repo — `swissarmyhammer-plugin`)
-The palette-open commands are ALREADY TS plugin commands (good — not Rust handlers), but there are **four** of them and the flag lives on the `ui_state` service, and the live frontend keymap binds a different key than the registry advertises:
+## Root cause (investigated): three-way divergence in the open path + no menu entry
+There are **three** palette-open command IDs, all wired to backend handlers, but the frontend keybindings only listen for one of them:

-| Command (TS plugin) | File | Keys | Routes to |
+| Command ID | Backend handler (`commands/mod.rs`) | Declared keys | Frontend listens? |
 |---|---|---|---|
-| `app.command` | `builtin/plugins/app-shell-commands/commands/app.ts:65` | vim `:`, cua/emacs `Mod+Shift+P` | `ui_state…command.show` (command mode) |
-| `app.palette` (visible:false) | `app.ts:81` | `Mod+Shift+P` all modes | `ui_state…palette.show` |
-| `app.search` | `app.ts:98` | vim `/`, cua/emacs `Mod+F` | `ui_state…search.show` (search mode) |
-| `ui.palette.open` | `builtin/plugins/ui-commands/index.ts:221` | cua `Mod+K`, vim `:` | `ui_state…palette.open` |
-| `ui.palette.close` | `ui-commands/index.ts:235` | — | `ui_state…palette.close` |
+| `ui.palette.open` | `PaletteOpenCmd` (mod.rs:150) | `ui.yaml`: cua **Mod+K**, vim `:` | ❌ NOT in `BINDING_TABLES`, NOT in any frontend scope |
+| `app.command` | `CommandPaletteCmd` (mod.rs:310) | `app.yaml`: vim `:`, cua/emacs **Mod+Shift+P** | ✅ vim `:` → `app.command` |
+| `app.palette` | `CommandPaletteCmd` alias (mod.rs:315) | **Mod+Shift+P** all modes | ✅ `Mod+Shift+P` → `app.palette` |

-The flag and its ops live on **`swissarmyhammer-ui-state`** (`crates/swissarmyhammer-ui-state/src/lib.rs`). `Mod+K` resolves to nothing in the running frontend because the live keymap only binds `Mod+Shift+P`/`:`/`/` — so the registry advertises a key the app never listens for, and no palette command carries a `menu:` placement (→ "not on the menu").
+- Frontend static `BINDING_TABLES` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts:27-140`) only bind `Mod+Shift+P → app.palette` (all modes) and vim `:` → `app.command`.
+- The frontend global command set (`STATIC_GLOBAL_COMMANDS`, app-shell.tsx:170-180) contains `app.command` and `app.palette` — but **NOT `ui.palette.open`**. So `extractScopeBindings` never surfaces `ui.palette.open`, and `Mod+K` is not in `BINDING_TABLES` → **`Mod+K` resolves to nothing in the live app.**
+- No palette command (`ui.palette.open` / `app.command` / `app.palette`) carries a `menu:` placement → **no native menu affordance at all** (matches "not on the menu").

-## DECIDED DESIGN (from user)
-1. **One and only one command** opens the command palette. Collapse `app.command`, `app.palette`, and `ui.palette.open` into a SINGLE command id (recommend keeping `ui.palette.open` as the survivor — it's the semantically correct, menu-world identity — and deleting `app.command` + `app.palette`). Open sub-decision: `app.search` opens the palette in *search* mode — keep it as a distinct command but it MUST route through the same single service flag (mode is a parameter, not a second flag). Flag this when implementing.
-2. **TS command registered as a plugin — never a Rust backend handler.** The survivor lives as a builtin TS plugin command (in `builtin/plugins/...`). Delete any remaining Rust `Command` impls for the palette (legacy `PaletteOpenCmd` / `CommandPaletteCmd` / `SearchPaletteCmd` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/{ui,app}_commands.rs`) so there is exactly one implementation path.
-3. **The `palette_open` flag lives on ONE plugin service — the `window` service** (`swissarmyhammer-window-service`), per user (open to alternatives; `ui_state` is the incumbent owner). Move per-window palette visibility (`palette_open` + `palette_mode`) out of `swissarmyhammer-ui-state` and onto the `window` service as a per-window field with `open palette` / `close palette` (+ mode) ops, so the single TS command and the React reader both read/write ONE service.
-   - Tradeoff to weigh during impl: `ui_state` already owns the other per-window UI-surface flags (inspector stack, keymap mode). Putting palette on `window` splits per-window UI state across two services. If that split is undesirable, the single-owner could instead be `ui_state` (which is already singular) — the hard requirement is ONE owning service, not which. Confirm window vs ui_state before moving state.
-4. **Wire the one command's key into the live keymap** so it actually fires (the root cause of "not by a hotkey"): the survivor's `keys` must drive the real keybinding handler — no separate static binding table that diverges. Pick ONE canonical shortcut and make registry + keymap + menu agree.
-5. **Add a `menu:` placement** to the survivor so there is a discoverable menu affordance (fixes "not on the menu").
+**Conclusion:** the registry/menu world advertises `ui.palette.open` / `Mod+K`, but the running frontend only opens the palette via **`Mod+Shift+P`** (or vim `:`). The user almost certainly pressed the advertised `Mod+K`, which is dead on the frontend.
+
+## First implementer step — bisect A vs B
+1. **A (most likely):** `Mod+Shift+P` DOES open the palette → the bug is the keybinding/menu divergence. Fix by consolidating to ONE palette command (prefer `ui.palette.open`), wiring its key into `BINDING_TABLES` / the frontend global scope, deleting the `app.command`/`app.palette` duplication, and adding a `menu:` placement (View or App) so there's a clickable affordance.
+2. **B (deeper):** if `Mod+Shift+P` ALSO does nothing, the failure is downstream — `palette_open` not propagating (`ui-state-context`), the `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` envelope not unwrapping (cf. the `{ok,change}` envelope issue in recent commit af9e6e965), or the `<FocusLayer parentLayerFq>` mount being rejected. Capture `log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"'` while pressing the key.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Exactly one command opens the command palette (the `app.command`/`app.palette`/`ui.palette.open` triplet is reduced to one; `app.search` only differs by mode and shares the single flag).
-- [ ] That command is a TS plugin command; no Rust `Command` impl for palette open/close remains.
-- [ ] `palette_open` (+ `palette_mode`) is owned by a single plugin service (window per user, or ui_state if the split is rejected) — not duplicated across services.
-- [ ] One canonical shortcut opens the palette in all keymap modes, and registry + live keymap + menu all agree on it.
-- [ ] A native-menu affordance opens the palette.
+- [ ] A single documented shortcut opens the palette in all keymap modes, and it matches what the registry/menu advertise (no Mod+K vs Mod+Shift+P divergence).
+- [ ] There is a discoverable native-menu affordance that opens the palette.
+- [ ] The three-way `ui.palette.open` / `app.command` / `app.palette` duplication is reconciled to a single source of truth (or intentional aliases that all share one binding wired into the frontend).
+- [ ] Root cause confirmed as A or B (see bisect step) and documented.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Update `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_app_shell_commands_e2e.rs` and `builtin_ui_commands_e2e.rs` for the collapsed command set (the current test asserts the old triplet incl. `app.palette` — it must change to assert the single survivor).
-- [ ] Service test on the chosen owner (`swissarmyhammer-window-service` or `swissarmyhammer-ui-state`): `open palette` sets the per-window flag and emits the change; `close palette`/`dismiss` clears it.
-- [ ] Frontend keymap test: the canonical shortcut resolves to the single palette command under the real board scope chain (no Mod+K vs Mod+Shift+P divergence).
-- [ ] Menu test: the palette command collects into its intended submenu.
+- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` so the canonical palette shortcut resolves to the canonical palette command under the real board scope chain.
+- [ ] A `menu.rs` test asserting the palette command collects into its intended submenu key (once a `menu:` placement is added).
+- [ ] If B: a `ui-state-context` test that a `PaletteOpen` change flips `windows[label].palette_open` and mounts the overlay.
 - [ ] Regression test failing before the fix, passing after.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — failing test first, then implement.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — failing test first, then fix.
\ No newline at end of file

Flag this when implementing.
+2. **TS command registered as a plugin — never a Rust backend handler.** The survivor lives as a builtin TS plugin command (in `builtin/plugins/...`). Delete any remaining Rust `Command` impls for the palette (legacy `PaletteOpenCmd` / `CommandPaletteCmd` / `SearchPaletteCmd` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/{ui,app}_commands.rs`) so there is exactly one implementation path.
+3. **The `palette_open` flag lives on ONE plugin service — the `window` service** (`swissarmyhammer-window-service`), per user (open to alternatives; `ui_state` is the incumbent owner). Move per-window palette visibility (`palette_open` + `palette_mode`) out of `swissarmyhammer-ui-state` and onto the `window` service as a per-window field with `open palette` / `close palette` (+ mode) ops, so the single TS command and the React reader both read/write ONE service.
+   - Tradeoff to weigh during impl: `ui_state` already owns the other per-window UI-surface flags (inspector stack, keymap mode). Putting palette on `window` splits per-window UI state across two services. If that split is undesirable, the single-owner could instead be `ui_state` (which is already singular) — the hard requirement is ONE owning service, not which. Confirm window vs ui_state before moving state.
+4. **Wire the one command's key into the live keymap** so it actually fires (the root cause of "not by a hotkey"): the survivor's `keys` must drive the real keybinding handler — no separate static binding table that diverges. Pick ONE canonical shortcut and make registry + keymap + menu agree.
+5. **Add a `menu:` placement** to the survivor so there is a discoverable menu affordance (fixes "not on the menu").
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] One canonical shortcut opens the palette in all keymap modes; registry + live keymap agree (no Mod+K vs Mod+Shift+P divergence).
-- [ ] Pressing the shortcut completes the open path and mounts the overlay.
-- [ ] `palette_open` (+ `palette_mode`) owned by a single service; `open`/`close`/`dismiss` flip it and emit the change.
-- [ ] No Rust `Command` impl for palette open/close remains (single TS plugin path).
+- [ ] Exactly one command opens the command palette (the `app.command`/`app.palette`/`ui.palette.open` triplet is reduced to one; `app.search` only differs by mode and shares the single flag).
+- [ ] That command is a TS plugin command; no Rust `Command` impl for palette open/close remains.
+- [ ] `palette_open` (+ `palette_mode`) is owned by a single plugin service (window per user, or ui_state if the split is rejected) — not duplicated across services.
+- [ ] One canonical shortcut opens the palette in all keymap modes, and registry + live keymap + menu all agree on it.
+- [ ] A native-menu affordance opens the palette.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Frontend keymap test: the canonical shortcut resolves to the single palette command under the real board scope chain.
-- [ ] Service test on the owning service: `open palette` sets the per-window flag + emits the change; `close`/`dismiss` clears it.
-- [ ] Overlay mount test: setting the flag mounts `<CommandPalette>`.
+- [ ] Update `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/builtin_app_shell_commands_e2e.rs` and `builtin_ui_commands_e2e.rs` for the collapsed command set (the current test asserts the old triplet incl. `app.palette` — it must change to assert the single survivor).
+- [ ] Service test on the chosen owner (`swissarmyhammer-window-service` or `swissarmyhammer-ui-state`): `open palette` sets the per-window flag and emits the change; `close palette`/`dismiss` clears it.
+- [ ] Frontend keymap test: the canonical shortcut resolves to the single palette command under the real board scope chain (no Mod+K vs Mod+Shift+P divergence).
+- [ ] Menu test: the palette command collects into its intended submenu.
 - [ ] Regression test failing before the fix, passing after.
